children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The term “dam” has the meaning given the term in .section 467 of title 33

children.heading: Inclusions
children.label: B Inclusions
children.text: The term “dam” includes any structure, facility, equipment, or vehicle used in connection with the operation of a dam.

children.label: A
children.text: the High-Hazard Indian Dam Safety Deferred Maintenance Fund established by subsection (b)(1)(A); or

children.label: B
children.text: the Low-Hazard Indian Dam Safety Deferred Maintenance Fund established by subsection (b)(2)(A).

children.heading: High hazard potential dam
children.label: 3 High hazard potential dam
children.text: The term “high hazard potential dam” means a dam assigned to the significant or high hazard potential classification under the guidelines published by the Federal Emergency Management Agency entitled “Federal Guidelines for Dam Safety: Hazard Potential Classification System for Dams” (FEMA Publication Number 333).

children.heading: Indian tribe
children.label: 4 Indian tribe
children.text: The term “Indian tribe” has the meaning given the term in .section 5304 of this title

children.heading: Low hazard potential dam
children.label: 5 Low hazard potential dam
children.text: The term “low hazard potential dam” means a dam assigned to the low hazard potential classification under the guidelines published by the Federal Emergency Management Agency entitled “Federal Guidelines for Dam Safety: Hazard Potential Classification System for Dams” (FEMA Publication Number 333).

children.heading: Secretary
children.label: 6 Secretary
children.text: The term “Secretary” means the Secretary of the Interior, acting through the Assistant Secretary for Indian Affairs, in consultation with the Secretary of the Army.

children.heading: Indian Dam Safety Deferred Maintenance Funds
children.label: b Indian Dam Safety Deferred Maintenance Funds
i
children.text: such amounts as are deposited in the Fund under subparagraph (B); and

children.label: ii
children.text: any interest earned on investment of amounts in the Fund under subparagraph (D).

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: For each of fiscal years 2017 through 2030, the Secretary of the Treasury shall deposit in the Fund $22,750,000 from the general fund of the Treasury.

children.heading: Availability of amounts
children.label: ii Availability of amounts
children.text: Amounts deposited in the Fund under clause (i) shall be used, subject to appropriation, to carry out this section.

children.label: II
children.text: the amount of interest accrued in the Fund.

children.heading: Additional expenditures
children.label: ii Additional expenditures
children.text: The Secretary may expend more than $22,750,000 for any fiscal year referred to in clause (i) if the additional amounts are available in the Fund as a result of a failure of the Secretary to expend all of the amounts available under clause (i) in 1 or more prior fiscal years.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: The Secretary of the Treasury shall invest such portion of the Fund as is not, in the judgment of the Secretary, required to meet current withdrawals.

children.heading: Credits to Fund
children.label: ii Credits to Fund
children.text: The interest on, and the proceeds from the sale or redemption of, any obligations held in the Fund shall be credited to, and form a part of, the Fund.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: The amounts required to be transferred to the Fund under this paragraph shall be transferred at least monthly.

children.heading: Adjustments
children.label: ii Adjustments
children.text: Proper adjustment shall be made in amounts subsequently transferred to the extent prior estimates are in excess of or less than the amounts required to be transferred.

children.label: i
children.text: the Fund shall terminate; and

children.label: ii
children.text: the unexpended and unobligated balance of the Fund shall be transferred to the general fund of the Treasury.

children.label: i
children.text: such amounts as are deposited in the Fund under subparagraph (B); and

children.label: ii
children.text: any interest earned on investment of amounts in the Fund under subparagraph (D).

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: For each of fiscal years 2017 through 2030, the Secretary of the Treasury shall deposit in the Fund $10,000,000 from the general fund of the Treasury.

children.heading: Availability of amounts
children.label: ii Availability of amounts
children.text: Amounts deposited in the Fund under clause (i) shall be used, subject to appropriation, to carry out this section.

children.label: II
children.text: the amount of interest accrued in the Fund.

children.heading: Additional expenditures
children.label: ii Additional expenditures
children.text: The Secretary may expend more than $10,000,000 for any fiscal year referred to in clause (i) if the additional amounts are available in the Fund as a result of a failure of the Secretary to expend all of the amounts available under clause (i) in 1 or more prior fiscal years.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: The Secretary of the Treasury shall invest such portion of the Fund as is not, in the judgment of the Secretary, required to meet current withdrawals.

children.heading: Credits to Fund
children.label: ii Credits to Fund
children.text: The interest on, and the proceeds from the sale or redemption of, any obligations held in the Fund shall be credited to, and form a part of, the Fund.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: The amounts required to be transferred to the Fund under this paragraph shall be transferred at least monthly.

children.heading: Adjustments
children.label: ii Adjustments
children.text: Proper adjustment shall be made in amounts subsequently transferred to the extent prior estimates are in excess of or less than the amounts required to be transferred.

children.label: i
children.text: the Fund shall terminate; and

children.label: ii
children.text: the unexpended and unobligated balance of the Fund shall be transferred to the general fund of the Treasury.

children.heading: Repair, replacement, and maintenance of certain Indian dams
children.label: c Repair, replacement, and maintenance of certain Indian dams
i
children.text: create flood risks or other risks to public or employee safety or natural or cultural resources; and

children.label: ii
children.text: unduly impede the management and efficiency of Indian dams.

children.heading: High-Hazard Fund
children.label: i High-Hazard Fund
children.text: Consistent with subsection (b)(1)(B), the Secretary shall use or transfer to the Bureau of Indian Affairs not less than $22,750,000 of amounts in the High-Hazard Indian Dam Safety Deferred Maintenance Fund, plus accrued interest, for each of fiscal years 2017 through 2030 to carry out maintenance, repair, and replacement activities for 1 or more of the Indian dams described in paragraph (2)(A).

children.heading: Low-Hazard Fund
children.label: ii Low-Hazard Fund
children.text: Consistent with subsection (b)(2)(B), the Secretary shall use or transfer to the Bureau of Indian Affairs not less than $10,000,000 of amounts in the Low-Hazard Indian Dam Safety Deferred Maintenance Fund, plus accrued interest, for each of fiscal years 2017 through 2030 to carry out maintenance, repair, and replacement activities for 1 or more of the Indian dams described in paragraph (2)(B).

children.heading: Compliance with dam safety policies
children.label: C Compliance with dam safety policies
children.text: Maintenance, repair, and replacement activities for Indian dams under this section shall be carried out in accordance with the dam safety policies of the Director of the Bureau of Indian Affairs established to carry out the Indian Dams Safety Act of 1994 ( et seq.).25 U.S.C. 3801

children.label: i
children.text: are included in the safety of dams program established pursuant to the Indian Dams Safety Act of 1994 ( et seq.); and25 U.S.C. 3801

children.label: aa
children.text: are owned by the Federal Government, as listed in the Federal inventory required by Executive Order 13327 ( note; relating to Federal real property asset management); and140 U.S.C. 121So in original. No cl. (ii) has been enacted.1

children.label: bb
children.text: are managed by the Bureau of Indian Affairs (including dams managed under contracts or compacts pursuant to the Indian Self-Determination and Education Assistance Act ( et seq.)); or25 U.S.C. 5301

children.label: II
children.text: have deferred maintenance documented by the Bureau of Indian Affairs.

children.label: i
children.text: are covered under the Indian Dams Safety Act of 1994 ( et seq.); and25 U.S.C. 3801

children.label: aa
children.text: are owned by the Federal Government, as listed in the Federal inventory required by Executive Order 13327 ( note; relating to Federal real property asset management); and40 U.S.C. 121

children.label: bb
children.text: are managed by the Bureau of Indian Affairs (including dams managed under contracts or compacts pursuant to the Indian Self-Determination and Education Assistance Act ( et seq.)); or25 U.S.C. 5301

children.label: II
children.text: have deferred maintenance documented by the Bureau of Indian Affairs.

children.label: i
children.text: would enable the completion of repairing, replacing, improving, or performing maintenance on Indian dams as expeditiously as practicable, subject to the dam safety policies of the Director of the Bureau of Indian Affairs established to carry out the Indian Dams Safety Act of 1994 ( et seq.);25 U.S.C. 3801

children.label: ii
children.text: facilitate or improve the ability of the Bureau of Indian Affairs to carry out the mission of the Bureau of Indian Affairs in operating an Indian dam; and

children.label: iii
children.text: ensure that the results of government-to-government consultation required under paragraph (4) be addressed; and

children.label: I
children.text: public or employee safety or health;

children.label: II
children.text: natural or cultural resources; or

children.label: III
children.text: the ability of the Bureau of Indian Affairs to carry out the mission of the Bureau of Indian Affairs in operating an Indian dam;

children.label: I
children.text: improve public or employee safety, health, or accessibility;

children.label: II
children.text: assist in compliance with codes, standards, laws, or other requirements;

children.label: III
children.text: address unmet needs; or

children.label: IV
children.text: assist in protecting natural or cultural resources;

children.label: iii
children.text: the methodology of the rehabilitation priority index of the Secretary, as in effect on ;December 16, 2016

children.label: iv
children.text: the potential economic benefits of the expenditures on job creation and general economic development in the affected tribal communities;

children.label: v
children.text: the ability of an Indian dam to address tribal, regional, and watershed level flood prevention needs;

children.label: vi
children.text: the need to comply with the dam safety policies of the Director of the Bureau of Indian Affairs established to carry out the Indian Dams Safety Act of 1994 ( et seq.);25 U.S.C. 3801

children.label: vii
children.text: the ability of the water storage capacity of an Indian dam to be increased to prevent flooding in downstream tribal and nontribal communities; and

children.label: viii
children.text: such other factors as the Secretary determines to be appropriate to prioritize the use of available funds that are, to the fullest extent practicable, consistent with tribal and user recommendations received pursuant to the consultation and input process under paragraph (4).

children.label: i
children.text: consult with the Director of the Bureau of Indian Affairs on the expenditure of funds;

children.label: ii
children.text: ensure that the Director of the Bureau of Indian Affairs advises the Indian tribe that has jurisdiction over the land on which a dam eligible to receive funding under paragraph (2) is located on the expenditure of funds; and

children.label: iii
children.text: solicit and consider the input, comments, and recommendations of the landowners served by the Indian dam.

children.heading: Emergencies
children.label: B Emergencies
children.text: If the Secretary determines that an emergency circumstance exists with respect to an Indian dam, subparagraph (A) shall not apply with respect to that Indian dam.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Subject to subparagraph (B), to the maximum extent practicable, the Secretary shall ensure that, for each of fiscal years 2017 through 2030, each Indian dam eligible for funding under paragraph (2) that has critical maintenance needs receives part of the funding under paragraph (1) to address critical maintenance needs.

children.label: i
children.text: more than 1 Indian tribe within an Indian reservation; or

children.label: ii
children.text: highly populated Indian communities, as determined by the Secretary.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: Subject to clause (ii), in allocating amounts under paragraph (1)(B), the Secretary shall allocate not more than $10,000,000 to any individual dam described in paragraph (2) during any consecutive 3-year period.

children.heading: Exception
children.label: ii Exception
children.text: Notwithstanding the cap described in clause (i), if the full amount under paragraph (1)(B) cannot be fully allocated to eligible Indian dams because the costs of the remaining activities authorized in paragraph (1)(B) of an Indian dam would exceed the cap described in clause (i), the Secretary may allocate the remaining funds to eligible Indian dams in accordance with this subsection.

children.heading: Basis of funding
children.label: D Basis of funding
children.text: Any amounts made available under this paragraph shall be nonreimbursable.

children.heading: Applicability of ISDEAA
children.label: E Applicability of ISDEAA
children.text: The Indian Self-Determination and Education Assistance Act ( et seq.) shall apply to activities carried out under this paragraph.25 U.S.C. 5301

children.heading: Tribal Safety of Dams Committee
children.label: d Tribal Safety of Dams Committee

children.heading: Establishment
children.label: A Establishment
children.text: The Secretary of the Interior shall establish within the Bureau of Indian Affairs the Tribal Safety of Dams Committee (referred to in this paragraph as the “Committee”).

children.label: aa
children.text: the Northwest Region;

children.label: bb
children.text: the Pacific Region;

children.label: cc
children.text: the Western Region;

children.label: dd
children.text: the Navajo Region;

children.label: ee
children.text: the Southwest Region;

children.label: ff
children.text: the Rocky Mountain Region;

children.label: gg
children.text: the Great Plans Region; and2So in original. Probably should be “Plains”.2

children.label: hh
children.text: the Midwest Region;

children.label: II
children.text: 2 shall be appointed by the Secretary of the Interior from among employees of the Bureau of Indian Affairs who have knowledge and expertise in dam safety issues and flood prevention and mitigation;

children.label: III
children.text: 1 shall be appointed by the Secretary of the Interior from among employees of the Bureau of Reclamation who have knowledge and expertise in dam safety issues and flood prevention and mitigation; and

children.label: IV
children.text: 1 shall be appointed by the Secretary of the Army from among employees of the Corps of Engineers who have knowledge and expertise in dam safety issues and flood prevention and mitigation.

children.heading: Nonvoting members
children.label: ii Nonvoting members
children.text: The members of the Committee appointed under subclauses (II) and (III) of clause (i) shall be nonvoting members.

children.heading: Date
children.label: iii Date
children.text: The appointments of the members of the Committee shall be made as soon as practicable after .December 16, 2016

children.heading: Period of appointment
children.label: C Period of appointment
children.text: Members shall be appointed for the life of the Committee.

children.heading: Vacancies
children.label: D Vacancies
children.text: Any vacancy in the Committee shall not affect the powers of the Committee, but shall be filled in the same manner as the original appointment.

children.heading: Initial meeting
children.label: E Initial meeting
children.text: Not later than 30 days after the date on which all members of the Committee have been appointed, the Committee shall hold the first meeting.

children.heading: Meetings
children.label: F Meetings
children.text: The Committee shall meet at the call of the Chairperson.

children.heading: Quorum
children.label: G Quorum
children.text: A majority of the members of the Committee shall constitute a quorum, but a lesser number of members may hold hearings.

children.heading: Chairperson and Vice Chairperson
children.label: H Chairperson and Vice Chairperson
children.text: The Committee shall select a Chairperson and Vice Chairperson from among the members.

children.heading: Study
children.label: A Study
children.text: The Committee shall conduct a thorough study of all matters relating to the modernization of the Indian Dams Safety Act of 1994 ( et seq.).25 U.S.C. 3801

children.heading: Recommendations
children.label: B Recommendations
children.text: The Committee shall develop recommendations for legislation to improve the Indian Dams Safety Act of 1994 ( et seq.).25 U.S.C. 3801

children.label: i
children.text: the Committee on Indian Affairs of the Senate; and

children.label: ii
children.text: the Committee on Natural Resources of the House of Representatives.

children.heading: Hearings
children.label: A Hearings
children.text: The Committee may hold such hearings, sit and act at such times and places, take such testimony, and receive such evidence as the Committee considers appropriate to carry out this paragraph.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: The Committee may secure directly from any Federal department or agency such information as the Committee considers necessary to carry out this paragraph.

children.heading: Request
children.label: ii Request
children.text: On request of the Chairperson of the Committee, the head of any Federal department or agency shall furnish information described in clause (i) to the Committee.

children.heading: Postal services
children.label: C Postal services
children.text: The Committee may use the United States mails in the same manner and under the same conditions as other departments and agencies of the Federal Government.

children.heading: Gifts
children.label: D Gifts
children.text: The Committee may accept, use, and dispose of gifts or donations of services or property.

children.heading: Non-Federal members
children.label: i Non-Federal members
children.text: Each member of the Committee who is not an officer or employee of the Federal Government shall be compensated at a rate equal to the daily equivalent of the annual rate of basic pay prescribed for level IV of the Executive Schedule under for each day (including travel time) during which the member is engaged in the performance of the duties of the Committee.section 5315 of title 5

children.heading: Federal members
children.label: ii Federal members
children.text: Each member of the Committee who is an officer or employee of the Federal Government shall serve without compensation in addition to that received for services as an officer or employee of the Federal Government.

children.heading: Travel expenses
children.label: B Travel expenses
children.text: The members of the Committee shall be allowed travel expenses, including per diem in lieu of subsistence, at rates authorized for employees of agencies under subchapter I of chapter 57 of title 5 while away from their homes or regular places of business in the performance of services for the Committee.

children.heading: Appointment
children.label: I Appointment
children.text: The Chairperson of the Committee may, without regard to the civil service laws and regulations, appoint and terminate an executive director and such other additional personnel as may be necessary to enable the Committee to perform the duties of the Committee.

children.heading: Confirmation
children.label: II Confirmation
children.text: The employment of an executive director shall be subject to confirmation by the Committee.

children.heading: Compensation
children.label: ii Compensation
children.text: The Chairperson of the Committee may fix the compensation of the executive director and other personnel without regard to chapter 51 and subchapter III of chapter 53 of title 5, relating to classification of positions and General Schedule pay rates, except that the rate of pay for the executive director and other personnel may not exceed the rate payable for level V of the Executive Schedule under section 5316 of that title.

children.heading: Detail of Government employees
children.label: D Detail of Government employees
children.text: Any Federal Government employee may be detailed to the Committee without reimbursement, and such detail shall be without interruption or loss of civil service status or privilege.

children.heading: Procurement of temporary and intermittent services
children.label: E Procurement of temporary and intermittent services
children.text: The Chairperson of the Committee may procure temporary and intermittent services under at rates for individuals that do not exceed the daily equivalent of the annual rate of basic pay prescribed for level V of the Executive Schedule under section 5316 of that title.section 3109(b) of title 5

children.heading: Termination of the Committee
children.label: 5 Termination of the Committee
children.text: The Committee shall terminate 90 days after the date on which the Committee submits the report under paragraph (2)(C).

children.heading: Funding
children.label: 6 Funding
children.text: Of the amounts authorized to be expended from either Fund, $1,000,000 shall be made available from either Fund during fiscal year 2017 to carry out this subsection, to remain available until expended.

children.heading: Tribal reports
children.label: 1 Tribal reports
children.text: The Secretary shall request that, not less frequently than once every 180 days, each Indian tribe submit to the Secretary a report providing an inventory of the dams located on the land of the Indian tribe.

children.heading: BIA reports
children.label: 2 BIA reports
children.text: Not less frequently than once each year, the Secretary shall submit to Congress a report describing the condition of each dam under the partial or total jurisdiction of the Secretary.

children.heading: Flood plain management pilot program
children.label: f Flood plain management pilot program
A
children.text: flood plain mapping; or

children.label: B
children.text: new construction planning.

children.heading: Termination
children.label: 2 Termination
children.text: The program shall terminate on the date that is 11 years after .December 16, 2016

children.heading: Funding
children.label: 3 Funding
children.text: Of the amounts authorized to be expended from either Fund, $250,000 shall be made available from either Fund during each of fiscal years 2017 through 2026 to carry out this subsection, to remain available until expended.
